name: Michael Frerichs
bio: Michael Frerichs is the incumbent Illinois State Treasurer, first elected in 2014 and running for a fourth term. He is a former state senator from Champaign County. As Treasurer, Frerichs oversees the state's investment portfolio and runs the I-Cash unclaimed property program, through which he has returned over $2 billion to Illinois residents and businesses. He is running unopposed in the Democratic primary. No Republican filed for the treasurer's race — making him effectively guaranteed re-election.
prior_office: Illinois State Treasurer (2015–present); Illinois State Senator

jacket.note: Unopposed in primary. No Republican filed — effectively guaranteed a fourth term. Best known for I-Cash unclaimed property program ($2B+ returned).

key_votes.summary: Administered Illinois's unclaimed property program, returning over $2 billion to residents and businesses over his tenure as Treasurer.

policy_positions: I-Cash unclaimed property returns to residents
Bright Start college savings program expansion
